content: We can reframe zero-shot node generalization on a unified interactive graph as the exploration objective of an ICM-driven autonomous agent, where the agent receives intrinsic reward for discovering and encoding unobserved out-of-distribution node types that the unified graph model has never seen during training. This turns the static task of zero-shot node generalization into an active, curiosity-driven exploration problem that leverages ICM's intrinsic reward to improve generalization on unseen node categories without external labeled data.
source_topics: Zero-shot node generalization on unified graph
trigger_topic: Zero-shot node generalization on unified graph
